title: Machine Learning for Protein Structure Prediction
description: Apply deep learning to predict protein 3D structures from amino acid sequences. Develop and train neural network models with AlphaFold-inspired architectures and analyze prediction accuracy across protein families.
requirements: Python, PyTorch, basic biology or biochemistry, 6.3900 or equivalent ML course

title: Autonomous Drone Navigation in GPS-Denied Environments
description: Develop SLAM algorithms for autonomous quadrotor navigation indoors and underground. Implement visual-inertial odometry and test on real drone hardware.
requirements: C++, ROS, linear algebra, 6.4210 or robotics experience

title: Natural Language Processing for Clinical Notes
description: Build NLP models that extract structured medical information from clinical notes to improve early cancer detection.

title: Reinforcement Learning for Robotic Manipulation
description: Develop RL algorithms for dexterous manipulation. Work on sim-to-real transfer and deploy policies on real robotic arms.
requirements: Python, PyTorch, RL basics (6.7900 or equivalent)

title: Computer Vision for Autonomous Vehicles
description: Work on 3D object detection and tracking for self-driving vehicles using LiDAR and real-world driving datasets.
requirements: Python, deep learning, point clouds or 3D data, 6.8300 or equivalent

title: Quantum Error Correction Simulations
description: Simulate quantum error-correcting codes on classical hardware. Implement surface code decoders and run Monte Carlo simulations.
requirements: Python, linear algebra, quantum mechanics (8.04 or equivalent)

title: Microfluidic Device Design for Cell Sorting
description: Design and fabricate microfluidic devices for high-throughput single-cell sorting. CAD design and soft lithography in the cleanroom.
requirements: CAD experience, chemistry or biology background, cleanroom willingness

title: Interpretable Deep Learning for Medical Imaging
description: Develop interpretable deep learning for brain MRI segmentation and attention visualization to increase clinician trust.
requirements: Python, PyTorch or TensorFlow, 6.8300 or computer vision

title: Differentially Private Machine Learning
description: Implement and evaluate differentially private training algorithms. Study privacy-accuracy tradeoff and DP-SGD variants.
requirements: Python, optimization and ML, 6.3900 or equivalent

title: Synthetic Biology and Genetic Circuits
description: Engineer genetic circuits in mammalian cells for therapeutic and biosensing applications. Design and test synthetic gene networks.
requirements: Molecular biology lab experience, 7.03 or equivalent, interest in synthetic biology

title: Cancer Immunotherapy and T Cell Engineering
description: Develop biomaterials and delivery strategies to enhance T cell therapy against solid tumors. In vitro and in vivo assays.
requirements: Immunology or cell biology, lab experience, 7.06 or equivalent

title: CRISPR Gene Drive Modeling in Mosquito Populations
description: Model spread of CRISPR-based gene drives through mosquito populations. Population genetics and agent-based simulations for malaria prevention.
requirements: Python or R, population genetics or ecology interest

title: Soft Robotics and Hydrogel Actuators
description: Design and test soft robotic actuators inspired by muscles. 3D printing with flexible materials and pneumatic control.
requirements: Mechanics or materials interest, hands-on prototyping

title: Ocean Circulation and Climate Modeling
description: Run high-resolution ocean circulation simulations. Study mesoscale eddies and their role in climate using MITgcm.
requirements: Python or MATLAB, fluid dynamics, HPC comfort, 12.003 or equivalent

title: Perovskite Solar Cell Fabrication
description: Fabricate and test perovskite solar cells. Spin-coat thin films, assemble devices, and measure photovoltaic performance.
requirements: Chemistry or materials background, lab safety training

title: Nanoparticle Drug Delivery for Cancer
description: Synthesize layer-by-layer nanoparticles for targeted cancer therapy. Cell viability assays and characterization (DLS, TEM).
requirements: Organic chemistry, 5.310 or equivalent lab course

title: Political Polarization and Social Media
description: Analyze social media datasets to study political polarization and information spread. Network analysis and causal inference.
requirements: Python or R, statistics, interest in social science

title: Traffic Signal Control with Multi-Agent RL
description: Develop multi-agent RL for traffic signal control. Build SUMO simulations and evaluate impact on flow and emissions.
requirements: Python, reinforcement learning or control, 6.7900 helpful

title: Urban Growth from Satellite Imagery
description: Apply computer vision to satellite imagery to track urban growth. Process multi-temporal data and create visualizations.
requirements: Python, remote sensing or GIS interest

title: High-Entropy Alloys with Machine Learning
description: Use ML to accelerate discovery of high-entropy alloys. Build models on DFT data and suggest compositions for validation.

title: Neural Circuits of Decision Making
description: Record and analyze neural activity in rodents during decision tasks. Investigate how brain represents time and choice.
requirements: Neuroscience or psychology, MATLAB or Python, 9.01 or equivalent

title: Developmental Biology and Stem Cells
description: Study epigenetic regulation in stem cells and early development. CRISPR-based editing and single-cell genomics.

title: Battery Materials and Electrochemistry
description: Design and characterize next-generation battery materials. Electrochemical testing and X-ray diffraction.

title: Fluid Dynamics of Insect Flight
description: Study unsteady fluid dynamics in insect flight and droplet breakup. High-speed imaging and flow visualization.

title: Archaeology and Ancient DNA
description: Extract and analyze ancient DNA from archaeological samples. Population genetics and ancestry inference.

title: Combinatorics and Graph Theory
description: Research in enumerative combinatorics, posets, and symmetric functions. Proof-based mathematics.

title: Sustainable Concrete and Construction Materials
description: Develop low-carbon concrete formulations and characterize mechanical properties. Life-cycle assessment.
requirements: Materials or civil engineering interest, 1.101 or 3.012

title: Microbiome and Host Health
description: Analyze microbiome data to link microbial communities to disease. Metagenomics and statistical modeling.
requirements: Python or R, microbiology or statistics, 7.03

title: Optics and Photonics for Sensing
description: Design optical systems and sensors. Fabricate and test photonic devices for imaging and communication.

title: Economic Development and Field Experiments
description: Design and analyze randomized controlled trials in education, health, or finance in developing countries.
requirements: Economics or statistics, 14.30 or 14.31, Stata or R

title: Prosthetics and Assistive Devices
description: Design and test prosthetic limbs and exoskeletons. Mechanical design, sensors, and human subject testing.

title: Chemical Synthesis of Natural Products
description: Total synthesis of biologically active natural products. Multi-step organic synthesis and characterization.

title: Astrophysics and Exoplanet Atmospheres
description: Model exoplanet atmospheres and interpret spectroscopic data. Contribute to mission planning for JWST or future missions.

title: Speech and Language Processing
description: Develop speech recognition and language understanding systems. Work with large speech corpora and neural models.

title: Tissue Engineering and Biomaterials
description: Engineer 3D tissue constructs for disease modeling. Cell culture, biomaterial scaffolds, and imaging.

title: Control Theory for Robotics
description: Develop control and planning algorithms for legged robots. Drake simulation and hardware experiments.
requirements: Linear algebra, 6.3020 or 6.4200, Python or C++

title: Environmental Justice and Policy
description: Model air quality and health impacts. Link emissions to policy and environmental justice outcomes.
requirements: Python or MATLAB, interest in policy and environment

title: Number Theory and Cryptography
description: Research in computational number theory, zero-knowledge proofs, and post-quantum cryptography.

title: Circadian Rhythms and Sleep
description: Analyze neural and behavioral data related to circadian rhythms and anesthesia. Statistical and dynamical models.
requirements: MATLAB or Python, statistics, neuroscience interest

title: Catalysis for Sustainable Chemistry
description: Design heterogeneous catalysts for CO2 conversion and green chemistry. Synthesis, characterization, and reactor testing.

title: History of Science and Technology
lab: Program in Science, Technology, and Society
description: Research the history of physics, computing, or biotechnology. Archival work and scholarly writing.
requirements: Strong writing, interest in history of science

title: Human-Robot Interaction
description: Design and evaluate social robots for education or healthcare. User studies and behavior design.
requirements: HCI or robotics interest, Python, 6.803 or 6.8340

title: Molecular Motors and Biophysics
description: Single-molecule studies of motor proteins and DNA mechanics. Optical tweezers and fluorescence.

title: Computational Social Science
description: Use mobile and sensor data to study human behavior and social networks. Privacy-preserving analytics.
requirements: Python, statistics, interest in social systems

title: Nuclear Fusion and Plasma Physics
description: Simulate and analyze fusion plasmas. Contribute to SPARC or other fusion experiments.

title: Plant Biology and Climate Resilience
description: Study epigenetic and genetic basis of stress response in plants. Molecular biology and genomics.

title: Robotic Assembly and Manipulation
description: Develop grasp planning and task-and-motion planning for robotic assembly. Drake and real robot hardware.
requirements: C++ or Python, 6.4210 or robotics course

title: Music and Audio Signal Processing
description: Design interactive music systems and analyze audio. Signal processing and creative technology.
requirements: 6.003 or music tech, Python or Max/MSP

title: Infectious Disease Modeling
department: Institute for Data, Systems, and Society
description: Build epidemiological models for disease spread. Agent-based or compartmental models and policy analysis.

title: Quantum Algorithms and Complexity
description: Research quantum algorithms, error correction, and complexity theory.

title: Structural Engineering and Mechanics
description: Model and test structural components. Finite element analysis and experimental mechanics.

title: Cognitive Development in Children
description: Run behavioral experiments with children to study learning and causal reasoning.

title: Polymer Science and Soft Matter
description: Design and characterize block copolymers and soft materials. Rheology and scattering techniques.

title: Wireless Networks and Sensing
description: Develop wireless systems for sensing and communication. RF and signal processing.

title: Marine Ecology and Conservation
description: Study nutrient cycling and microbial ecology in the ocean. Field work and lab measurements.

title: Computational Photography
description: Develop computational imaging and image processing algorithms. Cameras, optics, and software.
requirements: 6.8300 or image processing, Python or C++

title: Labor Economics and Education
description: Analyze education policy using quasi-experimental methods. Large-scale administrative data.

title: Additive Manufacturing of Metals
description: Study metal 3D printing processes and microstructure. Process monitoring and mechanical testing.

title: Vaccine Design and Immunology
description: Design vaccine formulations and delivery systems. In vivo immunization and immune assays.

title: Philosophy of Mind and AI
description: Study human and machine intelligence. Build computational models of perception and reasoning.
requirements: Cognitive science or AI, Python, 6.034 or 9.66

title: Geochemistry and Earth History
description: Use isotopic dating and geochemistry to study Earth history. Mass spectrometry and sample preparation.

title: Secure and Private Systems
description: Design operating systems and protocols for security and privacy. Implementation and verification.

title: Biomechanics of Movement
description: Model human locomotion and design assistive devices. Motion capture and dynamics.

title: Literary Data and Digital Humanities
description: Apply text analysis and NLP to literary and historical corpora.
requirements: Humanities interest, Python or R for text

title: Solar Energy and Photovoltaics
description: Characterize solar cell materials and improve efficiency. Optical and electrical measurements.

title: Behavioral Economics and Experiments
description: Design and run behavioral experiments on decision making. Data analysis and modeling.

title: Synthetic Ecology and Microbial Communities
description: Engineer microbial communities for biotech applications. Enrichment, sequencing, and modeling.

title: Computational Geometry and Graphics
description: Research geometry processing, shape analysis, and graphics. Algorithms and implementations.
requirements: Linear algebra, 6.006 or 18.06, C++ or Python

title: Atmospheric Chemistry and Air Quality
description: Model atmospheric composition and aerosol impacts. Use satellite and ground-based data.

title: Neural Interfaces and Brain-Computer Systems
description: Develop tools for recording and controlling neural activity. Optics, genetics, and electronics.

title: Supply Chain and Operations
description: Model supply chain resilience and logistics. Data analysis and optimization.

title: Organic Electronics and Flexible Devices
description: Synthesize and characterize conductive metal-organic frameworks and organic semiconductors.

title: Comparative Politics and Democratization
description: Research political institutions and development. Qualitative and quantitative methods.
requirements: Political science or related, 17.01, writing skills

title: Fluid-Structure Interaction
description: Model and test fluid-structure interaction for marine and aerospace applications.

title: Metabolic Engineering and Biosynthesis
description: Engineer metabolic pathways in bacteria and yeast for chemical production. Fermentation and analytics.

title: Algorithmic Fairness and Ethics
description: Study bias in algorithms and data. Develop fair ML and critical data studies.
requirements: Python or R, 6.3900 or data science, ethics interest

title: Thermal Systems and Energy Conversion
description: Design thermal management and energy conversion devices. Heat transfer and materials.

title: Linguistics and Language Acquisition
description: Study language processing and acquisition. Experiments and computational models.

title: Ceramic Materials for High Temperatures
description: Design and test ceramic and composite materials. Mechanical testing and microscopy.

title: Global Health and Implementation Science
department: Institute for Medical Engineering and Science
description: Evaluate health interventions in low-resource settings. Qualitative and quantitative data.
requirements: Public health or related, statistics, 14.31 or 18.05

title: Topological Materials and Condensed Matter
description: Theoretical and computational study of topological insulators and superconductors.

title: Urban Design and Spatial Analytics
description: Analyze urban form and mobility using GIS and spatial data.
requirements: GIS or Python, urban planning interest

title: Microbial Genomics and Evolution
description: Study bacterial evolution and horizontal gene transfer. Genomics and bioinformatics.

title: Robotics for Agriculture
description: Develop autonomous systems for farming. Sensing, planning, and field robots.

title: Art and Technology
description: Create installations and experiences at the intersection of art and technology.
requirements: Art or design background, programming or electronics

title: Statistical Mechanics and Complex Systems
description: Theoretical and simulation work on nonequilibrium statistical physics and self-organization.

title: Water Resources and Hydrology
description: Model hydrological cycles and remote sensing of soil moisture. Data assimilation.

title: Cancer Systems Biology
description: Model signaling networks in cancer. Combine experiments with computational models.

title: Architecture and Computation
description: Develop computational tools for architectural design and urban visualization.
requirements: Design or programming, Rhino/Grasshopper or Python

title: Magnetic Materials and Spintronics
description: Fabricate and characterize magnetic thin films and nanostructures.

title: Environmental Microbiology
description: Study marine and environmental bacteria. Metagenomics and culturing.

title: Game Theory and Mechanism Design
description: Research game theory, optimization, and mechanism design for networks and markets.

title: Medieval History and Manuscripts
description: Research medieval economic and social history. Paleography and archival sources.

title: Nanomedicine and Drug Delivery
description: Design polymeric and nanoparticle systems for controlled drug delivery.

title: Spacecraft Dynamics and Control
description: Develop estimation and control algorithms for satellites and space missions.

title: Organizational Behavior and Teams
description: Study team performance and collective intelligence. Experiments and surveys.

title: Coastal Engineering and Climate
description: Model coastal flooding and adaptation strategies. GIS and hydrodynamic models.
requirements: Civil or environmental interest, Python or GIS

title: Molecular Imaging and Probes
description: Design fluorescent and responsive molecules for sensing and imaging.

title: Discrete Mathematics and Algorithms
description: Research in property testing, sublinear algorithms, and randomized computation.

title: Climate Science and Carbon Cycle
description: Study atmospheric composition, climate feedbacks, and the carbon cycle. Data and models.
